title: caltha zetlandica
intro: Caltha zetlandica, also known as the Scottish marsh marigold, is a perennial plant species belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving rheumatic pain, and supporting the immune system. Caltha zetlandica can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and its roots and leaves are sometimes used in folk medicine, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
